skill.name: fastapi-templates
skill.description: Create FastAPI project templates with async routes, dependency injection, service layers, repositories, authentication examples, and testing patterns.
skill.summary: Build structured FastAPI API projects with async patterns, layered architecture, authentication, and tests.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, system-reconnaissance, and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from markdown fences and normal FastAPI examples. The skill is documentation-only, but its production template includes unsafe defaults such as wildcard CORS with credentials and SQL echo logging, so publication should include warnings. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration instructions were found.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Production CORS Defaults in Template
medium_findings.description: The FastAPI example sets wildcard allowed origins, methods, and headers while also enabling credentials. This is unsafe for a production template because generated APIs may accept cross-origin credentialed requests more broadly than intended.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code sample explicitly uses allow_origins=["*"] with allow_credentials=True in a template described as production-ready. This is a clear insecure default, although it is documentation and not executed by the skill itself.

medium_findings.title: Verbose Database Logging Enabled in Templates
medium_findings.description: The application and test database examples set SQLAlchemy echo logging to true. In production use, this can expose query details and sensitive values in logs if copied without modification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The echo=True setting is visible in database engine examples. The risk depends on deployment context, but the skill markets the example as production-ready.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings point to markdown code fences, resource links, or normal Python code examples. I found no executable installer, shell script, or instruction that runs host commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown delimiters or references inside documentation files. No shell command execution primitive is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Environment and Secret References Are Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: The database URL, secret key, and .env references are part of a FastAPI settings example. They do not read marketplace host secrets, but they require users to manage secrets securely in generated projects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variables are defined inside example application configuration and JWT code. They are legitimate FastAPI patterns, not evidence of exfiltration or hidden credential access by the skill.

low_findings.title: Network Alert Is Local Test Client Configuration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is the httpx test client base URL for in-process tests. It does not contact an external service or transmit credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is http://test inside an AsyncClient testing fixture. This is a normal local test pattern rather than network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Application Logic False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are SQL query execution, authorization comparisons, and JWT claim checks inside example application code. They do not inspect the host system or enumerate local resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are normal web application control flow and database query examples. No host reconnaissance commands, filesystem enumeration, or environment probing were found.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential access heuristic is not supported by context. The command alerts are markdown fences, the URL is a test base URL, and secret references are app configuration examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky primitives do not combine into executable behavior in the skill. I found no installer, network exfiltration target, obfuscation, or instruction to access marketplace host secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography, but the reviewed JWT sample uses HS256 with a configured secret and bcrypt for password hashing. No MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptography was found at the flagged locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations do not contain weak hash algorithms. HS256 is acceptable with strong secret management, but the template should still guide users to rotate and protect secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard CORS With Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: Production API examples should not combine broad origin policy with credentialed requests. Users should replace this with explicit trusted origins.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact CORS configuration is present in the main application sample. It is a risky default when copied into production projects.

dangerous_patterns.title: SQLAlchemy Echo Logging Enabled
dangerous_patterns.description: Database engine examples enable SQL echo logging. This can leak SQL statements and values through logs in deployed services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The engine examples explicitly include echo=True. This is acceptable for debugging but unsafe as a production default.

content.value_statement: Starting a FastAPI backend often requires repeated architecture decisions. This skill provides structured templates for async APIs, dependency injection, authentication, repositories, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a layered FastAPI project structure with routers, services, repositories, schemas, and core modules.
Provides async SQLAlchemy session and repository examples for CRUD workflows.
Shows service-layer patterns for user creation, password hashing, authentication, and updates.
Includes route examples that use FastAPI dependencies and typed request and response models.
Provides pytest and httpx async test fixture patterns for API endpoints.
Lists implementation best practices and common pitfalls for FastAPI projects.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not a generator that creates files automatically.
Examples must be reviewed before production use, especially CORS, logging, and secret handling.
It does not include complete referenced assets from the playbook resource list.
It does not replace framework documentation, dependency updates, or security review.

use_cases.title: Create a New API Skeleton
use_cases.description: Use the project layout to plan modules, routes, services, repositories, schemas, and core configuration before implementation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the layered examples to align a team on where routes, business logic, database access, and tests should live.

use_cases.title: Add Async Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Use the pytest and httpx examples to build endpoint tests with dependency overrides and in-memory database fixtures.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fastapi-templates skill to outline a new FastAPI project for a small REST API. Include the folder structure and explain each module briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Design Async CRUD Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fastapi-templates skill to design async CRUD layers for a FastAPI resource. Include schema, repository, service, and route responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Authenticated Endpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fastapi-templates skill to plan authenticated user endpoints. Include dependency injection, authorization checks, token validation, and safer production defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fastapi-templates skill to review my FastAPI project structure for production readiness. Focus on CORS, secrets, database logging, async correctness, and tests.

output_examples.input: Create a structure for a FastAPI inventory API.
output_examples.output: A module plan with api, core, models, schemas, services, repositories, and tests, plus notes on dependency injection and async database sessions.

output_examples.input: Add tests for a user creation endpoint.
output_examples.output: A testing approach using async pytest fixtures, an in-memory database, dependency overrides, and assertions for status and response fields.

output_examples.input: Review my FastAPI template for deployment risks.
output_examples.output: A checklist that calls out explicit CORS origins, disabled SQL echo logging, protected secrets, token settings, and integration test coverage.

best_practices: Replace wildcard CORS settings with explicit trusted origins before deployment.
Keep business logic in service classes and database access in repositories.
Use async database drivers, typed schemas, and integration tests for API behavior.
anti_patterns: Copying sample settings into production without reviewing CORS, logging, and secrets.
Putting database queries and business rules directly inside route handlers.
Mixing synchronous drivers into async routes and blocking the event loop.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full FastAPI application automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and examples that an AI coding tool can apply when creating project files.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the template safe for production as written?
faq.answer: Not without review. Update CORS origins, disable verbose database logging, and manage secrets securely before deployment.

faq.question: What architecture does it promote?
faq.answer: It promotes routers, dependency injection, services, repositories, Pydantic schemas, core configuration, and async tests.

faq.question: Does it cover authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes JWT token examples, password hashing examples, and current-user dependency patterns.

faq.question: Does it include deployment or infrastructure files?
faq.answer: No complete deployment assets are present in the scanned files. It mainly covers project architecture and implementation patterns.
